research.interface: Key Vault REST API api-version 2025-07-01: Get Secrets and Get Deleted Secrets list operations

official_sources.title: Get Secrets - Get Secrets - REST API (Azure Key Vault)
official_sources.used_for: GET {vaultBaseUrl}/secrets?api-version=2025-07-01 (the current default moniker), maxresults 1-25, SecretListResult{value, nextLink}, SecretItem{id, attributes, tags, contentType, managed}, SecretAttributes{enabled, created, updated, nbf, exp, recoveryLevel, recoverableDays}, the KeyVaultError{error{code, message, innererror}} shape, and the sample response whose nextLink carries a different api-version (7.2) proving the link is opaque

official_sources.title: Get Deleted Secrets - Get Deleted Secrets - REST API (Azure Key Vault)
official_sources.used_for: GET {vaultBaseUrl}/deletedsecrets?api-version=2025-07-01, DeletedSecretListResult paging, and DeletedSecretItem's recoveryId/deletedDate/scheduledPurgeDate fields on top of the SecretItem shape (id keeps pointing at /secrets/{name})

official_sources.title: Azure Key Vault throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: HTTP 429 as the throttle signal, the exponential backoff recommendation (1/2/4/8/16 seconds), and the note that throttled requests do not count against vault limits

official_sources.title: Authenticate to Azure Key Vault
official_sources.used_for: bearer-token authentication on every data-plane call, the 401 challenge behavior for missing/invalid tokens, and the forbidden (403) response when the firewall blocks the caller or the principal lacks the secrets/list permission

research.language_rationale: Python is the language of our existing vault tooling (kvclient.py). The inventory is built on the documented REST contract through the stdlib urllib stack the module already uses: azure-keyvault-secrets would add azure-core/azure-identity dependencies only to re-expose the same list operations, and the task's core behaviors (opaque nextLink following, distinct 401/403/429 mapping) are REST-level semantics the SDK would hide.

verified_facts: The current GA Key Vault secrets api-version is 2025-07-01; versions are date-based now, and the docs' own sample nextLink still carries api-version=7.2, so the continuation URL must be followed verbatim rather than rebuilt.
GET {vaultBaseUrl}/secrets lists only base secret identifiers and management attributes; individual versions and secret values are never part of the listing.
maxresults is optional with minimum 1 and maximum 25; the service returns up to 25 results per page when it is omitted.
SecretListResult carries value (SecretItem array) and nextLink; an absent or null nextLink means the listing is complete, and a page may be shorter than maxresults without being the last page.
SecretItem fields: id, contentType, managed, tags (string map), attributes{enabled, created, updated, nbf, exp, recoveryLevel, recoverableDays}; created/updated/nbf/exp are unix-time integers.
GET {vaultBaseUrl}/deletedsecrets uses the same paging shape; DeletedSecretItem adds recoveryId, deletedDate, and scheduledPurgeDate while id keeps the original /secrets/{name} form.

Key Vault errors arrive as {"error": {"code", "message", "innererror"}}; 401 means the token was missing/invalid (authentication), 403 means the authenticated principal lacks permission or the firewall refused the call, and 429 means throttling.
On 429 the documented client behavior is exponential backoff (1, 2, 4, 8, 16 seconds); Key Vault operations require the secrets/list permission for both list endpoints.
